content:
# Deuteronomy 4 — Disagreement Log
**Chapter:** Deuteronomy 4 (49 verses)
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA
**Total disagreements logged:** 20

---

## V.1 — "ordinances" vs. "judgments"

- **EMET:** "the statutes and the ordinances"
- **LOGOS:** "the statutes and the judgments"
- **PNEUMA:** "the statutes and the ordinances"
- **SOPHIA ruling:** "statutes and ordinances" (EMET/PNEUMA). The Hebrew *mishpatim* in Deuteronomic legal context is better rendered "ordinances"; "judgments" (LOGOS) implies more narrowly judicial decisions.

---

## V.4 — "held fast" vs. "cleave"

- **EMET:** "you who cleave to the LORD your God"
- **LOGOS:** "you who held fast to the LORD your God"
- **PNEUMA:** "all of you who held fast to the LORD your God"
- **SOPHIA ruling:** "you who held fast to the LORD your God" (LOGOS). "Held fast" is more natural English; PNEUMA's addition of "all of you" is not in the Hebrew at this verse.

---

## V.8 — "Torah" vs. "instruction"

- **EMET:** "this Torah"
- **LOGOS:** "this Torah"
- **PNEUMA:** "this instruction"
- **SOPHIA ruling:** "this instruction" (PNEUMA) — project convention: *torah* rendered as "instruction" in running text rather than transliterated.

---

## V.11 — "thick darkness" vs. "thick gloom"

- **EMET:** "darkness, cloud, and thick darkness"
- **LOGOS:** "darkness, cloud, and thick darkness"
- **PNEUMA:** "darkness, cloud, and thick gloom"
- **SOPHIA ruling:** "darkness, cloud, and thick darkness" (EMET/LOGOS). The Hebrew *arafel* = heavy storm-cloud/thick darkness. "Gloom" (PNEUMA) is thinner.

---

## V.19 — "led astray" vs. "drawn away"

- **EMET:** "and be led astray and bow down to them"
- **LOGOS:** "and be drawn away and bow down to them"
- **PNEUMA:** "do not be drawn away to bow down to them"
- **SOPHIA ruling:** "and be led astray and bow down to them" (EMET). "Led astray" (*nidachta* — driven/pushed away) captures the passive, seductive character of idolatrous temptation better than "drawn away."

---

## V.20 — "iron furnace" vs. "iron-smelting furnace"

- **EMET:** "the iron furnace"
- **LOGOS:** "the iron furnace"
- **PNEUMA:** "that iron-smelting furnace"
- **SOPHIA ruling:** "the iron furnace" (EMET/LOGOS). The Hebrew *kur ha-barzel* does not specify the smelting function — that is implicit in "furnace." PNEUMA adds "smelting" which is not in the MT.

---

## V.20 — "people of his own inheritance" vs. "people of his own possession"

- **EMET:** "a people of inheritance to him"
- **LOGOS:** "a people of his own possession"
- **PNEUMA:** "a people for his very own inheritance"
- **SOPHIA ruling:** "a people of his own inheritance." The Hebrew *am nahalah* — *nahalah* means inherited estate/patrimony. "Inheritance" (EMET/PNEUMA) is more precise than "possession" (LOGOS), which could apply to property acquired rather than inherited.

---

## V.22 — "I shall not cross" vs. "I am not crossing"

- **EMET:** "I am not crossing the Yarden"
- **LOGOS:** "I shall not cross the Jordan"
- **PNEUMA:** "I will not cross the Jordan"
- **SOPHIA ruling:** "I shall not cross the Jordan" (LOGOS). The modal "shall" carries the sense of a sealed divine decree, appropriate to the context of a sworn divine prohibition.

---

## V.29 — "with all your heart and with all your soul" vs. "heart and all your being"

- **EMET:** "with all your heart and with all your soul"
- **LOGOS:** "with all your heart and with all your soul"
- **PNEUMA:** "with all your heart and all your being"
- **SOPHIA ruling:** "with all your heart and with all your soul" (EMET/LOGOS). Shema-language (*be-khol levavkha u-ve-khol nafshekha*) must be consistent throughout the book. "Soul" for *nefesh* here.

---

## V.30 — "the latter days" vs. "the final days" vs. "in the end of days"

- **EMET:** "in the end of days"
- **LOGOS:** "in the latter days"
- **PNEUMA:** "in the final days"
- **SOPHIA ruling:** "in the latter days" (LOGOS). The Hebrew *be-acharit ha-yamim* is a temporally ambiguous phrase with eschatological usage in the prophets. "Latter days" preserves this without over-specifying.

---

## V.31 — "a merciful God" vs. "a compassionate God"

- **EMET:** "a merciful God [El rachum]"
- **LOGOS:** "a merciful God"
- **PNEUMA:** "a compassionate God"
- **SOPHIA ruling:** "a merciful God" (EMET/LOGOS). The root *racham* (womb-mercy) is traditionally rendered "merciful" in this divine epithet context. "Compassionate" (PNEUMA) is theologically equivalent but inconsistent with the project's established rendering of *rachum*.

---

## V.34 — "by a strong hand" vs. "by a mighty hand"

- **EMET:** "by a strong hand and by an outstretched arm"
- **LOGOS:** "by a mighty hand and an outstretched arm"
- **PNEUMA:** "with a mighty hand and an outstretched arm"
- **SOPHIA ruling:** "by a strong hand and an outstretched arm" (EMET). The Hebrew *yad chazaqah* = strong/firm hand — *chazaq* is the same root throughout Deuteronomy. Consistency with the established "strong hand" rendering is preferred over "mighty."

---

## V.35 — emphasis construction

- **EMET:** "You — you were shown to know that the LORD, he [is] God"
- **LOGOS:** "To you it was shown so that you might know that the LORD is God"
- **PNEUMA:** "You were shown all this so that you would know: the LORD — he is God"
- **SOPHIA ruling:** "To you it was shown so that you might know that the LORD — he is God" (LOGOS with EMET's emphasis particle restored). The emphatic pronoun *hu* (he is) should be reflected.

---

## V.37 — "with his own presence" vs. "himself, by his great power"

- **EMET:** "with his presence, with his great power"
- **LOGOS:** "with his own presence, by his great power"
- **PNEUMA:** "he brought you out of Egypt himself, by his great power"
- **SOPHIA ruling:** "with his own presence, by his great power" (LOGOS). The Hebrew *be-fanav* (by his face/presence) is theologically significant — it claims direct divine action without intermediary. LOGOS preserves both the "face/presence" term and the "great power" phrase.

---

## V.40 — "for all time" vs. "all the days"

- **EMET:** "all the days"
- **LOGOS:** "for all time"
- **PNEUMA:** "for all time"
- **SOPHIA ruling:** "for all time" (LOGOS/PNEUMA). The Hebrew *kol ha-yamim* — "all the days" — in this final clause of Moses' speech is best rendered "for all time" to capture the permanent nature of the gift.

---

## V.41 — "toward the sunrise" vs. "in the east"

- **EMET:** "toward the sunrise"
- **LOGOS:** "toward the sunrise"
- **PNEUMA:** "in the east"
- **SOPHIA ruling:** "toward the sunrise" (EMET/LOGOS). The Hebrew *mizrach ha-shemesh* = the rising of the sun — a vivid directional idiom that should not be collapsed to a cardinal point.

---

## V.42 — "without having been at enmity" vs. "without having hated him"

- **EMET:** "without having hated him in time past"
- **LOGOS:** "without being at enmity with him in time past"
- **PNEUMA:** "without having hated them beforehand"
- **SOPHIA ruling:** "without having been at enmity with him in time past" (LOGOS). The Hebrew *lo-sone' lo* — "not being a hater of him" — refers to premeditation. "At enmity" (LOGOS) is the most precise legal term. PNEUMA's "them" is a plural accommodation not in the Hebrew.

---

## V.44 — "instruction" vs. "Torah"

- **EMET:** "the Torah that Moshe placed before the sons of Yisra'el"
- **LOGOS:** "the Torah that Moses set before the children of Israel"
- **PNEUMA:** "the instruction Moses set before the sons of Israel"
- **SOPHIA ruling:** "the instruction that Moses set before the sons of Israel." Project convention: "instruction" for *torah*. "Sons of Israel" per project convention.

---

## V.45 — "ordinances" vs. "judgments"

- **EMET:** "the testimonies and the statutes and the ordinances"
- **LOGOS:** "the testimonies and the statutes and the judgments"
- **PNEUMA:** "the testimonies, statutes, and ordinances"
- **SOPHIA ruling:** "the testimonies and the statutes and the ordinances" (EMET/PNEUMA) — consistent with the ruling at v.1.

---

## V.48 — "Mount Sion" vs. "Mount Sirion/Siryon"

- **EMET:** "Mount Siryon"
- **LOGOS:** "Mount Sirion — that is, Hermon"
- **PNEUMA:** "Mount Sirion (that is, Hermon)"
- **SOPHIA ruling:** "Mount Sion — that is, Hermon." The MT reads *שִׂיאֹן* (Sion), not Siryon/Sirion. EMET, LOGOS, and PNEUMA all adopt the LXX/SP reading. SOPHIA follows the MT text despite the variant, noting that Sion here is identified with Hermon (distinguishing it from Zion/Jerusalem).
